answer:
All authentication for the monday.com GraphQL API at `https://api.monday.com/v2` is handled via token-based authentication [1][2]. Every request must include an access token provided in the `Authorization` header [1][2][3].

There are four primary ways to obtain an access token:

1. Personal API Tokens: Available via your profile under the Developers or Administration section [4][3].
2. OAuth: Used to issue access tokens with specific permission scopes [1][5].
3. Seamless Authentication (SDK): Used for view apps within iframes to avoid manual token management [1].
4. Short-lived Tokens: Provided for integration recipes, valid for five minutes [1][6].

Example using cURL:
```curl
curl -X POST https://api.monday.com/v2 \
  -H "Authorization: YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"query": "query { me { id name } }"}'
```

results.title: Does monday.com have an API? – Support
